journey.name: User Account & Authentication Flow
journey.description: End-to-end journey covering employee user creation, duplicate-username validation, listing all users, successful login with JWT issuance, and login failure with wrong credentials.

steps.name: Add user
steps.description: Create a new Apex Air employee user account with a randomly generated name, email, and username. Validates the full creation flow including password hashing and uniqueness checks.

expected.description: 201 Created — new employee account created. Response contains the new userId GUID. Password is stored as a SHA-256 hash; plain text is never persisted.
expected.validationNotes: userId is a non-empty GUID. username and email must be unique — attempting to create another account with the same values returns 409 Conflict (see Step 2). Name and email are runtime-generated values unique to this test run.

steps.name: Duplicate username attempt
steps.description: Attempt to create a second account using the same username generated in Step 1. Validates that the API correctly rejects the duplicate with a 409 Conflict response and does not modify the original account.

body.error: A user with this username already exists.

expected.description: 409 Conflict — the username from Step 1 is already registered. The original account is unaffected. Response body contains an error message.
expected.validationNotes: Uses the exact same username as Step 1 but a different email address, confirming that the username uniqueness constraint is enforced independently of email.

steps.name: Get all users
steps.description: Retrieve the full list of employee user accounts. The account created in Step 1 must appear in the results. Confirms that passwordHash is never returned in list responses.

expected.description: 200 OK — returns an array of all employee user accounts ordered by lastName then firstName. The account created in Step 1 must be present. passwordHash is never included.
expected.validationNotes: Response is always an array (empty array if no users exist). passwordHash must not appear on any object. isActive is true and isLocked is false for a freshly created account. lastLoginAt is null until the first successful login.

steps.name: Login — valid credentials
steps.description: Authenticate the employee account created in Step 1 using the runtime-generated username and password. Validates JWT issuance, account state update (LastLoginAt, FailedLoginAttempts reset), and correct response shape.

expected.description: 200 OK — credentials validated. Response contains a signed JWT access token (15-minute TTL), the userId, and the token expiry timestamp. LastLoginAt and FailedLoginAttempts are updated on the account.
expected.validationNotes: accessToken is a signed JWT (HMAC-SHA256). userId must match the value returned in Step 1. expiresAt is ISO 8601 UTC approximately 15 minutes from now. FailedLoginAttempts on the account is reset to 0.

steps.name: Login — wrong password
steps.description: Attempt to authenticate using the correct username from Step 1 but an incorrect password. Validates that the API returns 401 Unauthorized and increments the FailedLoginAttempts counter on the account.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — password does not match the stored hash. FailedLoginAttempts is incremented. After 5 consecutive failures the account is locked and further attempts return 403 Forbidden.
expected.validationNotes: The error message must not reveal whether the username exists (generic 'Invalid credentials' only). The account is not locked after a single failure — only after 5 consecutive failures.

steps.name: Delete user
steps.description: Delete the employee account created in Step 1 to clean up test data. Confirms that the DELETE endpoint permanently removes the account and returns 204 No Content.

expected.description: 204 No Content — the account created in Step 1 has been permanently deleted. No response body is returned. Subsequent requests to retrieve this userId return 404.
expected.validationNotes: Response body must be empty. The userId from Step 1 is injected into the path. Deleting a non-existent user returns 404.
